title: Status, Events, Logs, Metrics, Traces, Audit, and Layered Incident Response · Cluster Case: K8s Container Node Logs
summary: A release has green desired replicas but user failures, overwritten events, restarted containers, missing cluster logs, high-cardinality metrics, and no cross-layer correlation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s container node logs increment in cluster and workload incident evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Collect current and previous container logs, multi-container streams, timestamps, kubelet and runtime logs, rotation, retention, and node-loss boundaries.
Correlate spec, observedGeneration, conditions, reasons, messages, events, resource versions, controller ownership, and event retention without treating event order as durable history.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Container Node Logs · Theory K8s Container Node Logs
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory workload restarts after revision 4; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. Preserve object status, events, current and previous logs, metrics, traces, node evidence, and a causal timeline.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Container Node Logs contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example workload restarts after revision 4; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. Preserve object status, events, current and previous logs, metrics, traces, node evidence, and a causal timeline. Competency probe: Collect current and previous container logs, multi-container streams, timestamps, kubelet and runtime logs, rotation, retention, and node-loss boundaries. A defensible solution applies K8s Container Node Logs, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cluster and workload incident evidence record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-container-node-logs — Collect current and previous container logs, multi-container streams, timestamps, kubelet and runtime logs, rotation, retention, and node-loss boundaries.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-observability-debugging-incidents-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-observability-debugging-incidents-5
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked k8s observability debugging incidents 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      correlation: release-uid-request-trace
      status: generation-and-conditions
      events: timestamped-short-retention
      logs: current-previous-and-node
      metrics: resource-system-application
      audit: identity-verb-resource
      diagnosis: first-causal-layer
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pod,endpointslice -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous --timestamps
    failure: Reject green replica count as user proof, restart as diagnosis, events as durable history, kubectl logs as archival storage, or unbounded cardinality.
    repair: Preserve identity and time, correlate desired and observed layers, reproduce one fault cleanly, retain the regression, and document telemetry limits.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: kubectl logs is a durable cluster-wide log archive that survives Pod and node deletion. This fails the K8s Container Node Logs contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

    verify: kubectl get deployment,pod,endpointslice -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous --timestamps
    failure: Reject green replica count as user proof, restart as diagnosis, events as durable history, kubectl logs as archival storage, or unbounded cardinality.
